zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Roche於大約1385年出生在英國武斯特斯爾波維其可時，她的父親Sir John De La Roche29歲，母親Willelma De La Mare23歲。她和Sir Walter de Beauchamp至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1447年享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Roche于大约1385年出生在英国武斯特斯爾波維其可时，她的父亲Sir John De La Roche29岁，母亲Willelma De La Mare23岁。她和Sir Walter de Beauchamp至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1447年享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里。

nameMeanings.name: Roche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, Fionnuala, Kevin Patrick, Liam.
Irish (Munster) and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Roach  . Compare   Roch  .

French: topographic name for someone who lived by a rocky outcrop or crag, from Old French roche ‘rock’. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the variant Laroche 2. Compare Deroche .

variants.name: Laroche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Normand, Pierre, Jacques, Laurent, Adrien, Cecile, Germain, Marcel.
French:
habitational name from any of numerous places in various parts of France named Laroche or La Roche, from Old French roche ‘rock’, with feminine definite article la.

variants.name: Larocque
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Fernand, Henri, Marcel, Normand, Valmore, Yves, Achille, Amie, Donat, Emile.

French: habitational name from La Ro(c)que, the name of several places in various parts of France, from a regional variant of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche , compare Laroche ), with feminine definite article la. Compare Laroque .

Altered form of southern French Roquebrune or of its variant Rocquebrune: habitational name from Ro(c)quebrune, the name of several places in the southern part of France. The surname Roquebrune is rare in France, while the variant Rocquebrune is extinct there. Compare Larock , Lerocque , Rockburn , and Rocque 1.

variants.name: Rochet
meanings: French: diminutive of   Roche  , a topographic name meaning ‘rock’, or a habitational name from (Le) Rochet, the name of several places in various parts of France.
Catalan: of French origin (see above). This surname is also found in the Dominican Republic and Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Rochat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique, Philippe, Pierre.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of Old French roche ‘rock’ (see Roche 2), or a habitational name from Le Rochat, the name of several places in the eastern part of France.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Roquet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Camille.
French: topographic name from a diminutive of roche ‘rock’ (see Roche and Roque ), or a habitational name from (Le) Roquet, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Rochez
meanings: Walloon and French: variant of   Roche   2 or   Rocher  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Roche was born about 1385, in Powick, Worcestershire, England, her father, Sir John De La Roche, was 29 and her mother, Willelma De La Mare, was 23. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Walter de Beauchamp. She died in 1447, at the age of 63, and was buried in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, Unite: Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Roche est née environ 1385, à Powick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John De La Roche, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Willelma De La Mare, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Walter de Beauchamp. Elle est décédée en 1447, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Roche um 1385 in Powick, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John De La Roche 29 und ihre Mutter Willelma De La Mare 23. Sie hatte mit Sir Walter de Beauchamp mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1447 im Alter von 63 Jahren und wurde in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Roche è nata il circa 1385 nel Powick, Worcestershire, England, sua padre, Sir John De La Roche, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Willelma De La Mare, aveva 23 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Sir Walter de Beauchamp. Morì nel 1447, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolta a Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rocheが約1385年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアポウィックで生まれた時，父Sir John De La Rocheは29歳，母Willelma De La Mareは23歳であった。彼女はSir Walter de Beauchampとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1447年に63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Roche(이)가 대략 1385년 영국 워스스터실르 파윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John De La Roche(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Willelma De La Mare(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Sir Walter de Beauchamp(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1447년 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Roche nasceu aproximadamente 1385, em Powick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John De La Roche, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Willelma De La Mare, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Sir Walter de Beauchamp. Ela faleceu em 1447, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Roche родилась около 1385 г. в местности поуикк, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John De La Roche было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Willelma De La Mare, 23. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Sir Walter de Beauchamp. Она умерла в 1447 г. в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Roche nació aproximadamente 1385, en Powick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John De La Roche, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Willelma De La Mare, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Sir Walter de Beauchamp. Murió en 1447, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
